title: Chicken Fajita Bowls with Rice
description: This easy and flavorful chicken fajita bowl recipe is perfect for a busy weeknight, requiring minimal cleanup with only one or two pans. It's a kid-friendly meal that the whole family will love!

ingredients.item: Optional toppings: avocado, sour cream, cheese

instructions: Cook rice according to package directions. For extra flavor, use chicken broth instead of water.
While rice cooks, slice chicken breasts into thin strips. Slice bell peppers and onion into thin strips.
In a large bowl, toss chicken, bell peppers, and onion with olive oil and fajita seasoning until evenly coated.
Heat a large skillet or sheet pan over medium-high heat (or preheat oven to 400°F/200°C for sheet pan). Add chicken and vegetables, spreading in a single layer.
Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender-crisp. If using oven, roast for 15-20 minutes.
Once rice is cooked, fluff with a fork. Squeeze fresh lime juice over the chicken and vegetables, and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Assemble bowls by placing a scoop of rice, then topping with the chicken fajita mixture. Add optional toppings like avocado, sour cream, or shredded cheese.
tips: For picky eaters, you can serve the chicken and vegetables separately from the rice, or cut the vegetables into smaller pieces.
Make it a freezer meal by prepping the seasoned chicken and vegetables ahead of time and freezing them in a bag. Thaw and cook when ready.
Adjust the amount of fajita seasoning to your family's spice preference. For less heat, use a mild seasoning blend.
